items.body:
## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.AI.ContentUnderstanding` **Service directory:** `sdk/contentunderstanding/Azure.AI.ContentUnderstanding/` **Triggered by:** commit 6af7db6572976f4b6cc65bb628c45456114e88a0 by `@chienyuanchang` ## What Changed The 1.1.0 release (2026-04-21) added two new features to `Azure.AI.ContentUnderstanding`: 1. **`ContentUnderstandingClientSettings`** — a new `[Experimental("SCME0002")]` class that enables creating a `ContentUnderstandingClient` from `IConfiguration` / `IConfigurationSection`, supporting configuration-based credential resolution and ASP.NET Core dependency injection registration via `AddContentUnderstandingClient()`. 2. **`AnalyzeUsageDetails`** / **`GetUsage()`** — an extension method on completed analyze operations that surfaces billing and token consumption details (document page counts, contextualization tokens, per-model LLM/embedding token breakdown). ## Gaps Found - The README's **"Authenticate the client"** section only shows `DefaultAzureCredential` and `AzureKeyCredential` constructors. The new `ContentUnderstandingClient(ContentUnderstandingClientSettings)` constructor introduced in 1.1.0 is not documented anywhere in the README. - No sample file (`tests/samples/Sample*.cs` or `samples/Sample*.md`) exists for the `ContentUnderstandingClientSettings` / ASP.NET Core DI integration pattern. - The `AnalyzeUsageDetails` / `GetUsage()` feature is covered only in `samples/Sample03_AnalyzeInvoice.md` but is not mentioned in the README's **"Examples"** section listing or **"Key concepts"** section, making it undiscoverable to readers of the README. - The **"Key concepts"** / **"Main classes"** list does not mention `ContentUnderstandingClientSettings`.
📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files **File 1: `sdk/contentunderstanding/Azure.AI.ContentUnderstanding/README.md` — EDIT** In the **"Authenticate the client"** section (after the API key subsection and before the "Key concepts" section), add a new subsection: ```markdown #### Using IConfiguration and dependency injection (ASP.NET Core) > **Note:** This feature is experimental and subject to change. Opt in with `#pragma warning disable SCME0002`. If you use ASP.NET Core dependency injection, you can register `ContentUnderstandingClient` using `AddContentUnderstandingClient` and configuration: ```C# Snippet:CreateContentUnderstandingClientFromConfiguration ``` In your `appsettings.json`, add a section for the client settings: ```json { "ContentUnderstandingClient": { "Endpoint": "(yourfoundry.services.ai.azure.com/redacted) } } ``` ``` In the **"Main classes"** list inside **"Key concepts"**, add: ```markdown * **`ContentUnderstandingClientSettings`** — Settings class that allows creating a `ContentUnderstandingClient` from `IConfiguration`, enabling ASP.NET Core dependency injection integration ``` In the **"Examples"** section, update the list to include: ```markdown * **Usage Details** — Retrieve billing and token consumption details after analysis using `GetUsage()` (see `Sample03_AnalyzeInvoice.md`) * **Dependency Injection** — Register the client with ASP.NET Core dependency injection using `AddContentUnderstandingClient` ``` **File 2: `sdk/contentunderstanding/Azure.AI.ContentUnderstanding/tests/samples/SampleDI.cs` — CREATE** Create a new sample test file to back the new DI snippet: ```csharp // Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved. // Licensed under the MIT License. using System; using Azure.AI.ContentUnderstanding; using Azure.Core.Extensions; using Microsoft.Extensions.Azure; using Microsoft.Extensions.Configuration; using Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection; using Microsoft.Extensions.Hosting; namespace Azure.AI.ContentUnderstanding.Samples { public partial class ContentUnderstandingSamples { // Method kept for snippet extraction only; not run as a live test public void CreateContentUnderstandingClientFromConfiguration() { #region Snippet:CreateContentUnderstandingClientFromConfiguration #pragma warning disable SCME0002 IHostBuilder builder = Host.CreateDefaultBuilder(); builder.ConfigureServices((context, services) => { services.AddAzureClients(clients => { // Reads "ContentUnderstandingClient:Endpoint" from IConfiguration // and uses DefaultAzureCredential for authentication clients.AddContentUnderstandingClient(context.Configuration.GetSection("ContentUnderstandingClient")); }); }); #pragma warning restore SCME0002 #endregion } } } ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets The snippet name **`CreateContentUnderstandingClientFromConfiguration`** defined in `tests/samples/SampleDI.cs` (via `#region Snippet:CreateContentUnderstandingClientFromConfiguration` / `#endregion`) needs a corresponding placeholder in the README. **In `README.md`**, inside the new "Using IConfiguration and dependency injection" subsection, insert: ``` ```C# Snippet:CreateContentUnderstandingClientFromConfiguration ``` ``` (followed by a closing triple backtick). The `Update-Snippets.ps1` script will fill in the actual code from the test file's `#region` block. ### Step 3: Verify README structure The README already contains all required sections in order: 1. ✅ Getting started (Install the package, Prerequisites, Authenticate the client) 2. ✅ Key concepts 3. ✅ Examples 4. ✅ Troubleshooting 5. ✅ Next steps 6. ✅ Contributing No sections are missing; this change adds content within existing sections only. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order: 1. `dotnet build sdk/contentunderstanding/Azure.AI.ContentUnderstanding/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/contentunderstanding/Azure.AI.ContentUnderstanding/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 contentunderstanding` — injects the `CreateContentUnderstandingClientFromConfiguration` snippet into the README placeholder. Check the git diff after running to confirm README was updated correctly. 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 contentunderstanding` — no public API changes in this doc update, but run to confirm no regression. 5. `dotnet format sdk/contentunderstanding/Azure.AI.ContentUnderstanding/` — ensures consistent code formatting.
## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above
items.title: [ContentUnderstanding] Docs: Document IConfiguration/DI integration and usage details for 1.1.0
